channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Egypt lobbies against Trump plan to empty Gaza of Palestinians as Israel makes preparations
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel says it has begun preparations for the departure of Palestinians from Gaza despite international rejection of President Donald Trump’s plan to empty the territory of its population.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ukraine says its long-range drones hit a Russian airfield as France delivers Mirage fighter jets
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian forces struck an airfield in southern Russia that was being used to launch Iran-designed Shahed drones, Ukraine’s General Staff claimed Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel preparing for Palestinians' departure from Gaza after Trump remarks
item.description: Israel is preparing for a mass departure of Palestinian residents from Gaza after President Trump floated a proposal for the U.S. to take control of the strip currently under Hamas control.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Opposition lawmakers protest alleged mistreatment of Indian deportees by US
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — India’s Parliament was disrupted on Thursday as opposition lawmakers protested the alleged mistreatment of 104 Indian immigrants deported by the United States. A U.S.
item.dc:creator: AIJAZ HUSSAIN and CHONCHUI NGASHANGVA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Zelenskyy wants nukes or NATO; Trump special envoy Kellogg says 'slim and none' chance
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Kyiv should get NATO membership or nukes to defend against Russia as Trump's special envoy heads to Europe for talks with allies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The sea was once a blessing for the Pakistani city of Gwadar. But it's become a curse
item.description: GWADAR, Pakistan (AP) — There was a time when few people in the coastal Pakistani city of Gwadar understood what climate change was. After a decade of extreme weather, many more do. Rain battered Gwadar for almost 30 consecutive hours last February.
item.dc:creator: RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways as Trump’s Gaza Strip proposal reverberates
item.description: Aftershocks reverberated Wednesday from President Trump’s declaration the previous day that the United States should take “long-term ownership” of Gaza and that the Palestinians who live there should be moved.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, WGN 9

item.title: Argentina's president bans gender-affirming care for people under age 18
item.description: BUENOS AIRES (AP) — Argentina’s president has signed a decree banning gender-affirming care for people under age 18, a presidential spokesperson said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s demand that US aid workers return home sparks outrage in Washington and anxiety overseas
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Frustration boiled over Wednesday among supporters of the United States' lead aid agency at a Washington rally, and anxious aid workers abroad scrambled to pack up households after the Trump administration abruptly pulled almost all agency staffers off the job and out of the field.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, MATTHEW LEE and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte impeached, faces senate trial as political battle rages
item.description: Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte has been accused of various crimes, including corruption and plotting to assassinate the country's president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House: Trump hasn't committed to sending US military to Gaza
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt on Wednesday insisted President Trump has not committed to sending the U.S. military into Gaza after he appeared to float such a possibility during a joint press conference with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu the day before.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: US ally cautions world against doubting Trump's 'shockingly innovative' Gaza proposal
item.description: Hungarian Foreign Minister Szijjártó admits that President Donald Trump's proposals can be "shockingly innovative," but says that the world should not dismiss his Gaza Strip idea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: From fighting disease to protecting the Amazon rainforest, USAID has big impact across the globe
item.description: The Trump administration’s decision to close the U.S. Agency for International Development has drawn widespread criticism from congressional Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Kimberlee Kruesi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jeffries amps up call for two-state solution after Trump push to seize Gaza
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) on Wednesday flatly rejected President Trump’s proposal for the United States to take over the Gaza Strip, suggesting it would undermine ongoing efforts to free Israeli hostages and bring peace to the volatile region.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WGN 9

item.title: Jeffries amps up call for two-state solution after Trump push to seize Gaza
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) on Wednesday flatly rejected President Trump’s proposal for the United States to take over the Gaza Strip, suggesting it would undermine ongoing efforts to free Israeli hostages and bring peace to the volatile region.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, The Hill

item.title: US allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: When the president was asked about possibly sending in U.S. troops to Gaza, he said America will “do what is necessary.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: UN agencies raise alarm over Pakistan's move to deport thousands of Afghans waiting to go to the US
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — The U.N. refugees and migration agencies on Wednesday expressed their concern over Pakistan's decision to deport thousands of Afghan refugees awaiting relocation to the United States and elsewhere. The U.N.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: U.S. allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: Both U.S. allies and adversaries alike rejected President Donald Trump’s suggestion that the U.S. “take over” the war-torn Gaza Strip and turn it into the “Riviera of the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Iran's foreign minister responds to Trump 'maximum pressure' campaign amid regime panic
item.description: As President Donald Trump ramps up pressure on Iran, Tehran's foreign ministry announced a willingness to negotiate only the nuclear issue and not its support of terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico says it will not allow US to send Mexican migrants to Guantanamo Bay
item.description: Mexico told the the U.S. government it will not allow Mexican migrants to be sent to the Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, detention camp, the country's foreign minister said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Aga Khan, spiritual leader of Ismaili Muslims and a philanthropist, dies at 88
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The Aga Khan, who became the spiritual leader of the world’s millions of Ismaili Muslims at age 20 as a Harvard undergraduate and poured a material empire built on billions of dollars in tithes into building homes, hospitals and schools in developing countries, died Tuesday. He was 88.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Saudis could 'de-radicalize' Palestinians: Trump envoy
item.description: “We’ll have a lot of clarity for you in the coming days,” a Trump envoy says of questions about Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: At least 10 killed at adult education center in what officials say is Sweden's worst mass shooting
item.description: At least 10 people, including the gunman, were killed Tuesday at an adult education center in what Sweden’s prime minister called the country’s worst mass shooting. But a final death toll, a conclusive number of wounded and a motive hadn’t yet been determined hours later.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: White House flags top USAID boondoggles under Elon Musk's microscope
item.description: The White House has released a list of USAID programs its considers wasteful and that it says abused taxpayer funds by backing programs that supported DEI initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Denmark PM repeats Greenland ‘not for sale,’ but would welcome more American troops on Arctic island
item.description: Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen said she would welcome President Donald Trump sending more U.S. troops to Greenland, which is "not for sale.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Robert Levinson disappearance: FBI releases posters seeking info on two Iranian intelligence officers
item.description: The FBI is seeking information on two senior Iranian intelligence officers involved in the 2007 abduction of American Robert Levinson.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Uganda starts clinical trial of vaccine for Sudan strain of Ebola amid new outbreak
item.description: Uganda has begun a clinical trial of a vaccine against the Sudan strain of Ebola following an outbreak that has killed one nurse in the east African country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants rare earth elements from Ukraine in exchange for military aid
item.description: “We're looking to do a deal with Ukraine, where they're going to secure what we're giving them with their rare earth and other things,” he told reporters.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WGN 9

item.title: Emergency crews deployed on Santorini as earthquake swarm worries Greek experts
item.description: More than 200 undersea earthquakes have been detected near the Greek island of Santorini over the past three days, worrying Greek experts and island residents alike.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio visiting Central America with the Panama Canal and immigration top of mind
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio is on his first foreign trip in office, arriving in Central America on Saturday to press President Donald Trump's top priority — curbing illegal immigration — and to bring the message that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Venezuela frees 6 Americans after meeting between President Maduro and Trump's envoy
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Six Americans who had been detained in Venezuela in recent months were freed by the government of President Nicolás Maduro after he met Friday with a Trump administration official tasked with urging the authoritarian leader to take back deported migrants who have committed
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Arab nations reject Trump’s suggestion to relocate Palestinians from Gaza to Egypt and Jordan
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Powerful Arab nations on Saturday rejected U.S. President Donald Trump’s suggestion to relocate Palestinians from Gaza to neighboring Egypt and Jordan.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NASA's 2 stuck astronauts take their first spacewalk together
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — NASA’s two stuck astronauts took their first spacewalk together Thursday, exiting the International Space Station almost eight months after moving in.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, WGN 9
